name: Nuévalos - Monterde -
name_original: Nuévalos - Monterde - Cubel - Aldehuela de Liestos — circular desde El Monasterio de Piedra
description: This moderate 39.7-mile (63.9 km) road cycling route from El Monasterio de Piedra explores towns and scenic views in Aragon, Spain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Nuévalos navigate a landscape characterized by reservoirs, river valleys, and undulating terrain. The region features a mix of open roads and sections that follow historical paths, offering varied cycling experiences. Elevation gains are common, with routes often including significant climbs and descents. This area provides a setting for road cyclists seeking moderate to challenging rides. Best road cycling routes around Nuévalos The most popular road cycling route is Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop, a 28.4 miles (45.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route combines historical paths with views of the Tranquera Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Nuévalos - Monterde -, a moderate 39.7 miles (63.9 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain between the towns of Nuévalos and Monterde. Local road cyclists also love the Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) trail leading through reservoir and river valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Nuévalos is defined by reservoirs, river valleys, and hilly terrain. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Nuévalos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Nuévalos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Nuévalos?
A: Road cycling routes in Nuévalos are characterized by a diverse landscape featuring reservoirs, river valleys, and undulating terrain. You'll encounter a mix of open roads and sections that follow historical paths, often including significant climbs and descents, providing a varied and engaging cycling experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Nuévalos area?
A: There are 10 road cycling routes documented around Nuévalos, offering a range of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Nuévalos?
A: The road cycling routes around Nuévalos are generally suited for moderate to difficult ability levels. There are no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' in this guide, so beginners should be prepared for some challenges, including elevation gains.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in Nuévalos?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989969946/nuevalos-monterde" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuévalos - Monterde -&lt;/a&gt; route spans nearly 40 miles (63.9 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991320802/tranquera-reservoir-and-mesa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 37.4 miles (60.2 km) long. These routes offer substantial distances for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Nuévalos?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993007242/camino-del-cid-and-embalse-de-la-tranquera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991320802/tranquera-reservoir-and-mesa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What can I expect to see along the road cycling routes in Nuévalos?
A: The routes often feature scenic views of the Tranquera Reservoir and follow river valleys. You can also find natural attractions nearby, such as the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular highlights in the region.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Nuévalos?
A: The road cycling routes in Nuévalos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, the combination of historical paths, and the beautiful reservoir and river valley scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993007242/camino-del-cid-and-embalse-de-la-tranquera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, combines historical elements with views of the Tranquera Reservoir, offering a ride with cultural significance.

Q: What is the typical duration for a road cycling route in Nuévalos?
A: Route durations vary depending on length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993007242/camino-del-cid-and-embalse-de-la-tranquera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 11 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991320802/tranquera-reservoir-and-mesa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; often takes about 3 hours 4 minutes.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: Given that most routes are categorized as moderate to difficult with significant elevation gains, they may not be ideal for very young children or families seeking very easy, flat rides. Families with older, experienced cycling children might find some routes manageable, but it's best to review individual route profiles for specific details on elevation and terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Nuévalos?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, regions with undulating terrain and reservoirs like Nuévalos are generally best enjoyed in spring and autumn. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential cold of winter.
tips: We are in the Monasterio de la Piedra Natural Park (not free), an idyllic place for nature lovers where we will find waterfalls, lakes, caves, beautiful walkways and more. The building itself is an old restored Cistercian monastery from the 13th century, where we can see the transition from Romanesque to Cistercian Gothic and where its spectacular cloister stands out. It is important to check prices and schedules on the web: monasteriopiedra.com

Nice views of Nuévalos and the Tranquera reservoir in the background, from the road reaching the town. If we are lucky enough to see the reservoir with its well-charged water reserves, we will see its spectacular emerald blue color. We will find fishermen, people bathing and a lot of fauna.

As we pass through Monterde, we cannot miss the town's Castle located on top of the rocky hill where the remains of the square-plan tower that was part of a medieval fortress located in the Aragonese town are preserved. At present it is protected as an archaeological zone.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from El Monasterio de Piedra. You can typically find parking facilities available at or near the Monasterio de Piedra, which serves as a convenient starting and ending point for the loop.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is approximately 63.85 km long and generally takes around 3 hours and 12 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied and moderately challenging terrain, typical of the Aragon region. You can expect rolling hills with some significant ascents and descents, contributing to an elevation gain of about 740 meters. While primarily a racebike route, be aware that some sections in the broader Nuévalos area might include unpaved segments. It's advisable to check the exact road surface if you're looking for a purely paved experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll pass through charming Spanish towns and enjoy scenic views. Notable highlights include the stunning Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall, a fantastic View of Nuévalos and Tranquera Reservoir, and the historic Monterde Castle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences warm summers and cooler winters. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times for cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery without the intense heat of summer or the chill of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Monasterio de Piedra area?
faqs.answer: While cycling the route itself does not require a permit, accessing the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, which is a significant natural and historical site, typically requires an entrance fee. This fee grants access to the park's famous waterfalls, caves, and gardens. The cycling route passes by the park but does not enter it directly.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route spanning over 60 km, which is generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists, especially for the entire duration. While specific regulations for dogs on the public roads of the route may vary, it's best to consider this route as not dog-friendly for active participation.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You'll find small portions that overlap with trails such as Cimballa-Llumes, Llumes - Calmarza, Carretera Antigua, and the PR-Z 030.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, 63.85 km distance, and 740 meters of elevation gain, this route is best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness and some experience with road cycling. Beginners might find the ascents and overall distance quite challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse and picturesque landscape. You'll experience the natural beauty of the Spanish countryside, ranging from arid areas to more vegetated sections, especially around the river gorges. The views often include rolling hills, traditional Spanish villages, and glimpses of the stunning natural formations near the Monasterio de Piedra.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages like Nuévalos, Monterde, Cubel, and Aldehuela de Liestos. These towns may offer opportunities to refill water bottles, find cafes, or even small shops for snacks. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and provisions, especially on longer cycling routes in rural areas.

waypoints.name: View of Nuévalos and Tranquera Reservoir
